operation: First, create a 2D sketch on the XY workplane: start at coordinate (0, 0), draw a 30mm horizontal line to (30, 0), draw a three-point arc with midpoint at (40, 15) and endpoint at (30, 30), draw a 30mm horizontal line left to (0, 30), then draw a 30mm vertical line down to close the sketch. Extrude this closed profile 8mm along the positive Z axis to form the base solid. Next, select the topmost Z-facing face of the solid, create an aligned workplane on this face, generate a 2x2 rectangular point array with 10mm horizontal and 10mm vertical spacing, then cut 5mm diameter through holes at every array point.
geometry: This is a uniform-thickness flat prism whose base profile features three straight edges forming a squared left end, and a single convex curved edge that bulges outward at the right end. Four identical cylindrical through holes are arranged in a 2-row 2-column rectangular grid on the flat top face of the part, running fully through its thickness.
function: This is a compact low-profile mounting plate designed for mechanical assembly use, with pre-drilled grid mounting holes for fastener attachment and a curved end that fits rounded recesses or eliminates sharp contact edges in installations.
